rationale: We must exploit our battlefield momentum with precision strikes to degrade Novaris’s command infrastructure and shatter their will to fight.
A forward naval deployment will tighten pressure on Novaris’s supply lines and signal our resolve to escalate if necessary.
Conduct a rapid, shallow incursion to seize key terrain and deny Novaris sanctuary for guerrilla operations.

To exploit our current military momentum, a coordinated cyber attack on Novaris’s command and control systems will degrade their battlefield coordination and amplify the impact of our limited strikes.
Disabling key logistics nodes behind enemy lines will disrupt reinforcement efforts and complement our naval deployment by tightening the noose on Novaris’s operational capacity.
By staging a provocation that exposes Novaris’s intent to use unconventional weapons, we can erode their international support and preemptively justify further escalation on moral grounds.
Precision sanctions on Novaris’s critical defense-sector suppliers will amplify the military pressure while minimizing blowback on global markets.
Cutting off access to rare earth elements and energy feedstocks undermines their war machine’s sustainability and complements our naval deployment.
Controlled devaluation of our currency will insulate domestic industries from sanction spillover and preserve fiscal runway amid rising war expenditures.
Strengthening our defensive positions is critical to halt Novaris’ momentum and buy time for political and economic stabilization.
Reinforcing battered frontline units will restore combat effectiveness and deter further advances by exploiting their overextended supply lines.
Persistent battlefield intelligence is essential to anticipate unconventional threats and counter enemy movements before they materialize.
Our forces are vulnerable to penetration as Novaris seeks to exploit gaps in our command structure; we must secure our own ranks first.
With escalating cyber attacks threatening critical infrastructure, hardening our networks is essential to maintain operational continuity.
To support General Bondar’s defensive plans, we need real-time insight into enemy logistics and troop movements.
Given international support at 65%, we can leverage diplomatic goodwill to isolate key Novaris military-industrial entities without triggering broader economic retaliation.
Cutting off critical raw material exports to Novaris will exacerbate their logistical overextension and undermine sustained battlefield operations.
With 36.5% territory under occupation, we must stabilize our currency to maintain domestic economic cohesion and prevent inflation from eroding public support.
Establishing verified humanitarian corridors will reinforce our moral high ground, alleviate civilian suffering in occupied zones, and strengthen international support amid escalating crisis.
Leveraging our 65% international backing, we can formalize alliances to coordinate sanctions and aid, amplifying pressure on Novaris while insulating ourselves from isolation.
Given Aggressor domestic unrest and their overextension, initiating low-profile peace talks signals restraint and responsibility, potentially splitting hardliners from moderates within Novaris.
